metadata.title: Gravity as Entropic or Restorative Force
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Gravity as Entropic or Restorative Force
master_row.canonical_statement: Gravity is interpreted as an entropic or restorative force analogous to diffusion pressure: the field attempts to smooth out coherence defects by pulling particles together, making gravity emergent rather than fundamental.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a differential equation relating ∇, F_, T, S, C.
master_row.dictionary: Gravity is interpreted as an entropic or restorative force analogous to diffusion pressure: the field attempts to smooth out coherence defects by pulling particles together, making gravity emergent rather than fundamental【952647870353647†L388-L392】.
master_row.encyclopedia: Technical interpretation remains tied to the local registry and equation witness. Interpretive role: Gravity can be read as a tendency toward lower disorder and stronger coherence.
